REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION, TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION JUN-29-2023, IS GRANTED. UNTIL FURTHER ORDER OF THIS COURT DEFENDANT MUST CEASE ANY RENT/LICENSE/PAYING GUEST OCCUPANCY OF HER UNIT IN EXCHANGE FOR PAYMENTS FOR A TERM SHORTER THAN 30 DAYS. THIS INJUNCTION DOES NOT INCLUDE FAMILY MEMBERS AND FRIENDS WHO ARE NOT PAYING FOR THE STAY/OCCUPANCY. THIS ORDER SHALL BECOME EFFECTIVE UPON PLAINTIFF'S FILING OF A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $9,000. MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: CROSS COMPLAINANT DIANA MEISTRELL DEMURRER to ANSWER to CROSS COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. Amended Answer to Cross-Complaint on file. Judge: Sylvia L. Husing; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Defendant And Cross Complainant Diana Meistrells Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar May-19-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
G. Ryan Patrick, 415.552.8230 of Wiegel Law Gropu, PLC appearing for Defendant Diana Meistrell.
Kyle Withers, 415.713.8680 of Koster & Leadbetter appearing via Zoom for Plaintiff Maximilian McKelvy.

After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:
DEFENDANT DIANA MEISTRELL Defendant And Cross Complainant Diana Meistrells Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED, in part and DENIED, in part.
(1) Cross-complainant's request to enjoin Cross-defendant from installing interior bolt locks is denied. Cross-complainant has not clearly explained how she is being locked out of the premises and how a dead bolt is different/worse from the locks she used to replace the locks installed by Cross-defendant.
(2) Cross-complainant's request to enjoin Cross-defendant from continuing to use and maintain surveillance cameras in the common area is denied. The interim harm that Cross-complainant is likely to sustain if the injunction were denied is less compared to the harm that Cross-defendant is likely to suffer if the preliminary injunction were issued considering his legitimate safety concerns.
(3) Cross-complainant's request to enjoin Cross-defendant from storing personal property in the common area hallway and the garden/patio area is granted, in part. Cross-defendant is enjoined from storing his bicycle in the garden/patio. The request is otherwise denied. Aside from the exercise equipment, which consists of a stationary exercise bike, Cross-complainant fails to specify what other items Cross-defendant allegedly "stores" in the garden/patio. Cross-complainant also fails to explain how his furniture, but not hers, blocks access to the patio area.
(4) Cross-complainant's request to enjoin Cross-defendant from speaking to and/or making uninvited contact with Cross-complainant and her guests at the property is Denied. The request as presented in the moving parpers is avgue and over-broad. Mr. Patrick to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
